what: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
text: R package converts addresses coordinates (geocoding) assigns address district(s) falls within. works number district layers single run, can geocode member list attach several districting files (congressional, state house, county, etc.) time. two ways use , built functions: Shiny app — run_app(), point--click interface. R functions — call AssignDistricts() friends directly.

title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
text: local checkout:

title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
text: sidebar organized sections — Member data, District files, Options, Download — matching steps . Upload member list (.csv, .xlsx, .xls) addresses want assign. (Excel files require suggested readxl package.) Select Address City columns. default app looks columns named Street.Address City; aren’t found can pick columns use geocoding. Geocode Addresses. Press geocode member list. Geocoding independent district files, can run soon columns chosen — gather district files. status line reports many addresses matched, result cached isn’t repeated unnecessarily. (skip step, geocoding runs automatically assign districts.) Add district files. Upload district geometry .gpkg .geojson files directly, .zip archives (required .shp, needs sidecar files). button adds files list, can keep adding many like; file can removed individually ✕ card. Give one name — name becomes prefix layer’s output columns (e.g. layer named Congressional turns DISTRICT column Congressional_DISTRICT), keeps columns different layers distinct. Assign Districts. Assigns geocoded address district(s) falls within, across uploaded layers. reuses cached geocoding, runs (fast) spatial joins. Review results. summary run appears, results show table per-column filters Show/hide columns button taming wide results (long cell text truncated — hover see full value). Rows couldn’t geocoded highlighted red carry geocode_status Missing address (blank/NA address), geocode match (address found), Geocoder error (geocoding request failed — e.g. network hiccup — clearing geocode cache re-running may fix ). Geocodes low match score (geo_score 85) highlighted amber — matched, possibly wrong place, give second look. Nothing dropped — every input row appears output. Map tab (requires suggested leaflet package) shows results map: district polygons labeled per-district member counts, points colored assignment layer pick, gray points addresses outside every district, count table underneath. Download. Download section opens results exist, original columns district assignments pre-selected (geocoder bookkeeping columns — original_row_id, geocode_status, geo_* — offered unchecked). Adjust selection like (“Select ” / “Select none” links included) export .csv. Additional options: Retry failed geocodes US Census geocoder (default ) gives addresses ArcGIS geocoder can’t find second chance free US Census Bureau geocoder (US addresses ). Recovered rows marked geo_source = "Census". Remove Geometry Column (default ) drops spatial geometry output clean CSV export. Restrict geocoding district area (default ) limits geocoding area district files cover. Leave normal use; turn want exclude matches far outside region. Clear geocode cache forces next run geocode scratch. Clear district files empties district list. run_app(max_upload_mb = 50) raises 20 MB upload limit.

title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
text: geocode apply many districting files efficiently, split two steps — addresses geocoded single time reused: default geocoding unrestricted (depends address + city). limit district area, pass restrictToDistrictArea = TRUE AssignDistricts(), build extent compute_search_extent() pass GeocodeMembers(). Every result includes geocode_status column carries summary statistics attribute; print_summary(result) writes console.
code: library(DistrictAssignments)  # One-shot: geocode and assign in a single call. Use a NAMED list to control the # output column prefixes. result <- AssignDistricts(   memberList    = "members.csv",   StreetCol     = "Street.Address",   CityCol       = "City",   districtsList = list(     Congressional = "shapes/congress.shp",     StateSenate   = "shapes/senate.geojson"   ) ) # -> columns include Congressional_DISTRICT, StateSenate_DISTRICT, and a #    geocode_status column flagging any "Missing address" / "No geocode match". members  <- read.csv("members.csv") layers_a <- prepare_district_layers("shapes/congress.shp",  "Congressional") layers_b <- prepare_district_layers("shapes/senate.geojson", "StateSenate")  pts <- GeocodeMembers(members, "Street.Address", "City")  # geocode once  result_a <- AssignToDistricts(pts, layers_a) result_b <- AssignToDistricts(pts, layers_b)

title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
text: Every input row kept. Rows can’t geocoded flagged, removed, via geocode_status (OK / Missing address / geocode match / Geocoder error). Rows stay matched. Geocoding results aligned exact source row, blank unmatched address never shifts rows. Points outside every district stay blank. geocoded address doesn’t fall inside polygon layer simply gets NA layer’s columns (plots gray app’s map). Large lists geocoded chunks (500 addresses per request batch), transient service failure affects chunk: rows flagged Geocoder error (retryable — census fallback still gets chance ) instead whole run aborting. Layer names made unique. number district layers can used ; duplicate names (e.g. two files named congress) disambiguated (congress, congress_1) every layer keeps distinct, predictable output columns. input column already name layer’s output column (say, re-uploaded export carrying Congressional_DISTRICT), input copy renamed _input suffix fresh assignment keeps expected name. Overlapping polygons resolved first matching district per layer (note), point never duplicated multiple rows. Boundary points assigned. default point lying exactly district boundary matches (join_type = "intersects"); point touching two districts resolves first, note. Pass join_type = "within" require points strictly inside district (boundary points get NA). Reserved columns. original_row_id, geocode_status, geo_* columns rewritten GeocodeMembers(). input CSV already (e.g. re-upload previous export), replaced fresh values stale results can’t leak new run.

title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
text: District geometry must .shp, .geojson, .gpkg (GeoPackage), must carry coordinate reference system (shapefiles, keep .prj sidecar). multi-layer GeoPackage uses first layer, note. Geocoding uses R package arcgisgeocode (ArcGIS World Geocoder), optionally falling back US Census Bureau geocoder failed addresses (censusFallback = TRUE; US ). street address city sent geocoding service(s); columns processed locally. default geocoding restricted area. Enabling restriction uses (padded) combined extent uploaded district layers. Spatial joins planar (s2 disabled), appropriate model administrative district polygons.

title: Geocode Member Lists and Assign Addresses to Districts
code: devtools::load_all()   # load the package devtools::test()       # run the test suite (offline; one live geocode test                        # runs only when NOT_CRAN=true and a connection exists) devtools::check()      # full R CMD check

what: Geocode a member list and assign it to districts in one call — AssignDistricts
title: Geocode a member list and assign it to districts in one call — AssignDistricts
text: One-shot convenience wrapper around prepare_district_layers(), GeocodeMembers(), AssignToDistricts(). "geocode , apply many districting files" workflow, call GeocodeMembers() AssignToDistricts() repeatedly instead.

title: Geocode a member list and assign it to districts in one call — AssignDistricts
code: AssignDistricts(   memberList,   StreetCol = "Street.Address",   CityCol = "City",   districtsList,   removeGEO = TRUE,   districtNames = NULL,   restrictToDistrictArea = FALSE,   boundaries = NULL,   join_type = c("intersects", "within"),   censusFallback = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Geocode a member list and assign it to districts in one call — AssignDistricts
text: memberList data.frame, path .csv / .xlsx / .xls file, addresses (Excel files require suggested readxl package). StreetCol Name street-address column. CityCol Name city column. districtsList single sf layer file path, character vector file paths, (optionally named) list sf layers /file paths (.shp / .geojson / .gpkg). sf objects pass ; paths read disk. removeGEO Drop geometry column result (TRUE = clean CSV export). districtNames Optional character vector names, one per layer, used prefix layer's columns. restrictToDistrictArea TRUE, geocoding limited (padded) union extent district layers. default – see compute_search_extent() . Ignored boundaries supplied. boundaries Optional explicit geocoding search extent (bbox). Overrides restrictToDistrictArea. Default NULL = restriction. join_type point must relate polygon assigned. "intersects" (default) also matches points lying exactly district boundary (boundary point touching two districts resolves first, message); "within" requires point strictly inside, boundary points receive NA. censusFallback TRUE, addresses primary (ArcGIS) geocoder fails retried free US Census Bureau geocoder (US addresses ). Recovered rows get coordinates, status "OK", geo_source = "Census". Default FALSE. verbose Print progress summary console.

title: Geocode a member list and assign it to districts in one call — AssignDistricts
text: See AssignToDistricts().

title: Geocode a member list and assign it to districts in one call — AssignDistricts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- AssignDistricts(   memberList    = "members.csv",   StreetCol     = "Street.Address",   CityCol       = "City",   districtsList = list(     Congressional = "shapes/congress.shp",     StateSenate   = "shapes/senate.geojson"   ) ) } # }

what: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
title: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
text: Assign already-geocoded points (output GeocodeMembers()) one PREPARED district layers (output prepare_district_layers()). function prepare/prefix layers , callers reusing geocoded points across many layer sets pay preparation .

title: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
code: AssignToDistricts(   geocoded,   layers,   removeGEO = TRUE,   join_type = c("intersects", "within"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
text: geocoded Output GeocodeMembers() (rows, geo_x/geo_y geocode_status). layers list prepared sf layers (see prepare_district_layers()). removeGEO Drop geometry column result (TRUE = clean CSV export). join_type point must relate polygon assigned. "intersects" (default) also matches points lying exactly district boundary (boundary point touching two districts resolves first, message); "within" requires point strictly inside, boundary points receive NA. verbose Print messages multiply-matched points.

title: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
text: data.frame (sf object removeGEO = FALSE) one row per input member, original order, district layers' prefixed columns joined . Summary statistics attached "summary_stats" attribute; see print_summary().

title: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
text: Every input row carried : rows without coordinates become empty points simply receive NA district columns, geocoded point falls outside every polygon layer receives NA layer. Supports number layers; overlapping polygons within layer resolve first match (message) point never duplicated multiple rows. input column name layer's prefixed output column (e.g. re-uploaded previous export still carrying Congressional_DISTRICT), input copy renamed _input suffix (message) fresh assignment keeps expected name.

title: Assign geocoded points to district layers — AssignToDistricts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pts    <- GeocodeMembers("members.csv", "Street.Address", "City") layers <- prepare_district_layers("shapes/congress.shp", "Congressional") result <- AssignToDistricts(pts, layers) } # }

title: Geocode a member list — GeocodeMembers
text: Geocode member list return every input row, flagged (possible) coordinated. rows dropped. Splitting geocoding assignment enables "geocode , apply many districting files" workflow: geocode member list single time, reuse points across number layer sets via AssignToDistricts().

title: Geocode a member list — GeocodeMembers
code: GeocodeMembers(   memberList,   StreetCol,   CityCol,   boundaries = NULL,   censusFallback = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Geocode a member list — GeocodeMembers
text: memberList data.frame, path .csv / .xlsx / .xls file, addresses (Excel files require suggested readxl package). StreetCol Name street-address column. CityCol Name city column. boundaries Optional bbox (see compute_search_extent()) restrict geocoding. Default NULL = unrestricted. censusFallback TRUE, addresses primary (ArcGIS) geocoder fails retried free US Census Bureau geocoder (US addresses ). Recovered rows get coordinates, status "OK", geo_source = "Census". Default FALSE. verbose Print progress console.

title: Geocode a member list — GeocodeMembers
text: member data.frame columns added/replaced: original_row_id stable 1..N identifier mapping results back Street.Address, City resolved address/city used geocoding geocode_status "OK", "Missing address" (blank street/city), "geocode match" (geocoder found candidate), "Geocoder error" (request failed – e.g. network/service problem – address never actually tried) geo_x, geo_y longitude/latitude (NA geocoded) geo_source service located row: "ArcGIS", "Census" (fallback), NA geocoded geo_* additional geocoder fields (geo_score, geo_match_addr, ...)

title: Geocode a member list — GeocodeMembers
text: columns original_row_id, geocode_status, everything prefixed geo_ reserved: (re)written function. input already contains (e.g. re-uploaded previous export), replaced freshly computed values stale results can never leak new run. address city columns coerced character (column read numeric still geocodes), requests sent chunks 500 large lists transient service failure affects chunk: rows flagged "Geocoder error" (warning naming row range) instead aborting run, can recovered re-running census fallback.

title: Geocode a member list — GeocodeMembers

what: Compute a geocoding search extent from district layers — compute_search_extent
title: Compute a geocoding search extent from district layers — compute_search_extent
text: Union bounding box layers, reprojected WGS84 (EPSG:4326), suitable passing geocoder search extent. Using union (rather just one layer) means box covers TOTAL area districts span.

title: Compute a geocoding search extent from district layers — compute_search_extent
Compute a geocoding search extent from district layers — compute_search_extent
text: layers list prepared sf layers (see prepare_district_layers()). pad Fraction box's width/height added every side (default 5%). Keeps edge addresses falsely failing geocode.

title: Compute a geocoding search extent from district layers — compute_search_extent
text: sf::st_bbox() EPSG:4326.

title: Compute a geocoding search extent from district layers — compute_search_extent
text: Geocoding search extent opt-(see AssignDistricts()'s restrictToDistrictArea). default search extent hard-limits candidates inside box: valid address just outside every district return candidate mis-flagged geocode failure, really "found, just unassigned".

what: Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers
title: Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers
text: Normalize, read, validate name-prefix one district layers ready hand AssignToDistricts().

title: Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers
Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers
text: districtsList single sf layer file path, character vector file paths, (optionally named) list sf layers /file paths (.shp / .geojson / .gpkg). sf objects pass ; paths read disk. districtNames Optional character vector names, one per layer, used prefix layer's columns.

title: Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers
text: list prepared sf layers validated geometry, coordinate reference system, name-prefixed attribute columns.

title: Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers
text: layer's attribute columns prefixed layer's name (e.g. DISTRICT column layer named Congressional becomes Congressional_DISTRICT) columns different layers stay distinct joined output. Names resolved per layer, order precedence: explicit districtNames entry, name districtsList element, file's base name (path elements), positional fallback. Duplicate names made unique (message), number layers – including several files sharing base name – keep distinct, predictable output columns. Layers without CRS without features rejected clear error; layer containing polygons triggers warning.

title: Prepare district layers for assignment — prepare_district_layers

what: Print the summary statistics of an assignment result — print_summary
title: Print the summary statistics of an assignment result — print_summary
text: Prints summary attached AssignToDistricts() / AssignDistricts() result console: total, invalid, failed, successful address counts, plus specific rows failed geocode.

title: Print the summary statistics of an assignment result — print_summary
Print the summary statistics of an assignment result — print_summary
text: result return value AssignToDistricts() AssignDistricts().

title: Print the summary statistics of an assignment result — print_summary
text: result, invisibly.

what: Launch the district-assignment Shiny app — run_app
title: Launch the district-assignment Shiny app — run_app
text: point--click interface GeocodeMembers() AssignToDistricts(): upload member CSV, geocode , add number district files (.gpkg, .geojson, zipped .shp), review results table map, download assigned results. Requires suggested packages shiny, DT, bslib; map tab additionally uses leaflet (hint shown installed).

title: Launch the district-assignment Shiny app — run_app
Launch the district-assignment Shiny app — run_app
text: max_upload_mb Maximum upload size megabytes (default 20). ... Passed shiny::runApp() (e.g. port, launch.browser).

title: Launch the district-assignment Shiny app — run_app
text: Runs app; return app live.

title: Launch the district-assignment Shiny app — run_app
DistrictAssignments 0.1.9
text: Bug-fix internal-cleanup release addressing batch review findings: district-guessing heuristic, map’s “using column” picker, geometry-removal download now work correctly, supporting internals consolidated unit-tested.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.9
text: District-column guessing now recognizes TIGER congressional codes (CD118FP-style); previously files fall back OBJECTID. map’s “using column” picker longer goes blank re-running Assign Districts, switching layers longer redraws map twice. “Remove Geometry Column” works : unchecking now adds geometry_wkt column (WKT text) CSV download. results table never displays now says .

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.9
text: district-guessing heuristic moved package internals now covered unit tests. Results flattened plain data frame source instead every consumer dropping sf geometry . District layers now geometry-repaired exactly , every input path — 0.1.8 removed double repair file-path inputs, app uploads still paid . results map longer reprojects every district layer redraw; layers transformed per Assign run.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.8
text: Bug-fix release: clean output geometry kept, map identifies districts correctly real-world files.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.8
text: Downloading “Remove Geometry Column” unchecked longer writes bogus geometry column stringified points (e.g. c(-97, 30)) CSV, results table longer renders . sf geometry column sticky [ selection, now dropped columns chosen table download. results map longer identifies districts layer’s first attribute column, real district files (e.g. Census TIGER, whose leading column STATEFP/OBJECTID/Shape_Area) collapsed every point one color tallied members irrelevant field. now picks district-like column (DISTRICT, NAMELSAD, CD, SLDU/SLDL, WARD, PRECINCT, GEOID, …) name, new “using column” dropdown lets override guess per selected layer point coloring member-count table.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.8
text: prepare_district_layers() longer repairs geometry twice file-path inputs (read_spatial_file() already repairs read); extra st_make_valid() pass now runs directly-supplied sf layers still need . .DS_Store now git-ignored untracked.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.7
text: App UI overhaul: interface now stays usable many district files, many columns, long district lists.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.7
text: sidebar reorganized accordion sections (Member data, District files, Options, Download) bslib::page_sidebar() layout, primary “Assign Districts” button styled placed prominently. Download section opens automatically results exist. Lists grow data capped scroll areas instead stretching sidebar: per-file district naming list download column picker. uploaded district file now shows compact card file name truncated (full name hover) remove button, single bad upload longer requires clearing re-adding everything. download column picker defaults original member columns plus district assignments (geocoder bookkeeping columns – original_row_id, geocode_status, geo_* – offered unchecked), gains “Select ” / “Select none” links. results table shows 25 rows, adds “Show/hide columns” button per-column filters, truncates long cell text 40 characters full value shown hover. post-run summary appears modal dialog instead notification; notification collapsed line breaks one long paragraph. map’s per-district member count table paged searchable, layer hundreds districts can’t stretch page; map’s layer control collapses 5 layers loaded. street/city column selectors searchable server-side (selectize, server = TRUE), member files hundreds columns stay fast. Requires bslib >= 0.6.1 app.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.6
text: Production-hardening release: large member lists, many district layers, messy input can longer sink run.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.6
text: Geocoding requests now sent chunks 500 per-chunk error handling. Previously single failed request (timeout, rate limit, server error) aborted entire geocode call – list thousands addresses, one transient failure lost everything. Now affected chunk’s rows left ungeocoded, warning naming row range. New geocode_status value "Geocoder error" marks rows whose geocode request failed (retryable), distinguishing "geocode match" (address didn’t resolve). counted separately summary, shown app’s status line, announced app notification explaining retry; census fallback still retries . Street/city columns coerced character geocoding, column Excel CSV read numeric (house numbers, ZIP-like values) longer aborts run type error. Duplicate district layer names made unique (message). Previously two layers name – e.g. two uploaded files named congress – produced colliding output columns sf silently renamed .x/.y, also broke app’s map coloring count table. Layer names sanitize nothing (e.g. "!!!") now fall back positional District_N prefix instead leaving columns unprefixed. Input columns collide layer’s output columns (typically re-uploaded previous export still carrying Congressional_DISTRICT) renamed _input suffix, message, fresh assignment always lands documented column name. District layers features rejected clear error (assign NA everywhere previously made restrictToDistrictArea = TRUE fail invalid extent); compute_search_extent() also validates extent finite. layer containing polygons (e.g. points file uploaded mistake) triggers warning. census fallback now stops 5 consecutive request failures instead burning 30-second timeout every remaining row service unreachable, prints progress every 100 rows large retry batches. Failed-address listings (console summary app notification) capped 10 rows “… N ” note, instead flooding console screen thousands rows fail. member list row geocodes longer emits confusing “non-missing arguments max” warnings assignment.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.5
text: Census geocoder fallback: GeocodeMembers() AssignDistricts() gain censusFallback argument (default FALSE; app’s checkbox defaults ). Addresses ArcGIS geocoder fails retried free US Census Bureau geocoder; recovered rows get coordinates, status "OK", geo_source = "Census". new geo_source column records service located row. Results map: app’s main panel now Table/Map tabset. map (built suggested leaflet package) shows every district layer’s polygons toggleable groups labeled per-district member counts, plots geocoded points colored assignment selectable layer. Points outside every district plot gray; rows without coordinates counted caption. per-district member count table appears map. prepare_district_layers() now names returned list resolved layer names. Assigned results now retain geo_x/geo_y coordinate columns (previously consumed spatial join), exports include coordinates map can plot .

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.0
text: First release R package (previously set standalone scripts).

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.0
text: run_app() launches Shiny interface; command-line workflow exported functions AssignDistricts(), GeocodeMembers(), prepare_district_layers(), AssignToDistricts(), compute_search_extent(), print_summary(). Points lying exactly district boundary now assigned (join_type = "intersects", default); pass join_type = "within" previous strict behavior. Member lists can Excel files (.xlsx / .xls) well CSV, app GeocodeMembers() (requires suggested readxl package). District layers can GeoPackage files (.gpkg) well .shp .geojson; app accepts .gpkg .geojson uploads directly, without zipping. app highlights low-confidence geocodes (geo_score 85) amber results preview.

title: DistrictAssignments 0.1.0
text: Re-uploading previous export longer lets stale geo_x/geo_y coordinates survive rows fail geocoding: original_row_id, geocode_status, geo_* columns reserved rewritten every run. geocoder’s nested extents field longer carried results, broke CSV export. District name inputs app keyed upload, clearing district list adding new files longer inherits names typed earlier files. character vector layer paths, layer CRS, NA district names now work fail clear messages instead confusing errors. Spatial joins always planar (s2 disabled, caller’s setting restored), results longer depend file formats read earlier session.
